-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-5x23","title":"Add ratings and reviews schema tables","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-7x3.

## Task
Add ratings and reviews tables for community content quality signals.

## Schema Definition
```typescript
// Content types that can be rated
type RatableContentType = "user_prompt" | "prompt_pack" | "skill";

export const ratings = pgTable("ratings", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  userId: uuid("user_id").notNull().references(() => users.id),
  
  // What is being rated
  contentType: text("content_type", { 
    enum: ["user_prompt", "prompt_pack", "skill"] 
  }).notNull(),
  contentId: uuid("content_id").notNull(),
  
  // Rating value: 1 (thumbs down) or 5 (thumbs up) for MVP
  // Can expand to 1-5 scale later
  ratingValue: integer("rating_value").notNull(),  // 1 or 5 for now
  
  // Timestamps
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow().notNull(),
  updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow().notNull(),
}, (table) => [
  index("idx_ratings_content").on(table.contentType, table.contentId),
  index("idx_ratings_user").on(table.userId),
  unique("uq_user_rating").on(table.userId, table.contentType, table.contentId),
]);

// Reviews (V2 - text reviews with ratings)
export const reviews = pgTable("reviews", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  ratingId: uuid("rating_id").notNull().references(() => ratings.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
  userId: uuid("user_id").notNull().references(() => users.id),
  
  // Review content
  content: text("content").notNull(),
  
  // Moderation
  status: text("status", { enum: ["pending", "approved", "rejected"] })
    .default("approved").notNull(),
  
  // Timestamps
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow().notNull(),
  updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow().notNull(),
}, (table) => [
  index("idx_reviews_rating").on(table.ratingId),
]);

// Denormalized rating stats for fast queries
export const ratingStats = pgTable("rating_stats", {
  contentType: text("content_type", { 
    enum: ["user_prompt", "prompt_pack", "skill"] 
  }).notNull(),
  contentId: uuid("content_id").notNull(),
  
  // Aggregated stats
  totalRatings: integer("total_ratings").default(0).notNull(),
  positiveRatings: integer("positive_ratings").default(0).notNull(),  // thumbs up
  negativeRatings: integer("negative_ratings").default(0).notNull(),  // thumbs down
  approvalRate: real("approval_rate"),  // positive / total * 100
  
  lastCalculatedAt: timestamp("last_calculated_at", { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
}, (table) => [
  primaryKey({ columns: [table.contentType, table.contentId] }),
]);
```

## Triggers/Functions
- On rating insert/update/delete: recalculate rating_stats
- Approval rate = (positive / total) * 100

## Notes
- MVP uses binary thumbs up/down (stored as 5 and 1)
- rating_stats provides fast reads without aggregation
- One rating per user per content item

Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-7x3.

## Task
Add ratings and reviews tables for community content quality signals.

## Schema Definition
```typescript
// Content types that can be rated
type RatableContentType = "user_prompt" | "prompt_pack" | "skill";

export const ratings = pgTable("ratings", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  userId: uuid("user_id").notNull().references(() => users.id),
  
  // What is being rated
  contentType: text("content_type", { 
    enum: ["user_prompt", "prompt_pack", "skill"] 
  }).notNull(),
  contentId: uuid("content_id").notNull(),
  
  // Rating value: 1 (thumbs down) or 5 (thumbs up) for MVP
  // Can expand to 1-5 scale later
  ratingValue: integer("rating_value").notNull(),  // 1 or 5 for now
  
  // Timestamps
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow().notNull(),
  updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow().notNull(),
}, (table) => [
  index("idx_ratings_content").on(table.contentType, table.contentId),
  index("idx_ratings_user").on(table.userId),
  unique("uq_user_rating").on(table.userId, table.contentType, table.contentId),
]);

// Reviews (V2 - text reviews with ratings)
export const reviews = pgTable("reviews", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  ratingId: uuid("rating_id").notNull().references(() => ratings.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
  userId: uuid("user_id").notNull().references(() => users.id),
  
  // Review content
  content: text("content").notNull(),
  
  // Moderation
  status: text("status", { enum: ["pending", "approved", "rejected"] })
    .default("approved").notNull(),
  
  // Timestamps
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow().notNull(),
  updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow().notNull(),
}, (table) => [
  index("idx_reviews_rating").on(table.ratingId),
]);

// Denormalized rating stats for fast queries
export const ratingStats = pgTable("rating_stats", {
  contentType: text("content_type", { 
    enum: ["user_prompt", "prompt_pack", "skill"] 
  }).notNull(),
  contentId: uuid("content_id").notNull(),
  
  // Aggregated stats
  totalRatings: integer("total_ratings").default(0).notNull(),
  positiveRatings: integer("positive_ratings").default(0).notNull(),  // thumbs up
  negativeRatings: integer("negative_ratings").default(0).notNull(),  // thumbs down
  approvalRate: real("approval_rate"),  // positive / total * 100
  
  lastCalculatedAt: timestamp("last_calculated_at", { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
}, (table) => [
  primaryKey({ columns: [table.contentType, table.contentId] }),
]);
```

## Triggers/Functions
- On rating insert/update/delete: recalculate rating_stats
- Approval rate = (positive / total) * 100

## Notes
- MVP uses binary thumbs up/down (stored as 5 and 1)
- rating_stats provides fast reads without aggregation
- One rating per user per content item

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-a7ms","title":"Create Terms, Privacy Policy, and Guidelines pages","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-qc8.

## Task
Create legal pages for Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, and Community Guidelines.

## Pages

### /terms (Terms of Service)
Key sections:
1. Acceptance of Terms
2. Description of Service
3. User Accounts
4. Subscription and Billing
5. User Content and Ownership
6. Prohibited Conduct
7. Intellectual Property
8. Disclaimer of Warranties
9. Limitation of Liability
10. Termination
11. Governing Law
12. Contact Information

### /privacy (Privacy Policy)
Key sections:
1. Information We Collect
   - Account data (email, name from Google)
   - Content you create
   - Usage data
2. How We Use Information
3. Information Sharing (Stripe, Supabase, analytics)
4. Data Retention
5. Your Rights (access, deletion, export)
6. Cookies
7. Children's Privacy
8. Changes to Policy
9. Contact

### /guidelines (Community Guidelines)
Key sections:
1. Be Respectful
2. Prohibited Content
   - Spam
   - Malware/harmful prompts
   - Copyright infringement
   - Hate speech
   - Personal information
3. Enforcement
4. Reporting
5. Appeals

## Implementation
- MDX files for easy editing
- Last updated date display
- Table of contents sidebar
- Print-friendly styling
- Version in footer (v1.0)

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-akwo","title":"CLI cross-repo dependency matrix","description":"## Task
Add a CLI cross-repo dependency matrix to keep public CLI work aligned with premium APIs.

## Why This Matters
- Prevents drift between CLI client and premium backend
- Clarifies which premium endpoints power each CLI command
- Speeds up planning and reduces integration bugs

## Matrix (Public -> Premium)
- jeffreysprompts.com-uw7z (CLI login)
  - premium device code endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-0o1
  - premium verification page: jeffreysprompts_premium-qdm
  - premium CLI auth page: jeffreysprompts_premium-mn0
  - premium JWT/token utils: jeffreysprompts_premium-qnl
  - premium refresh/revoke: jeffreysprompts_premium-0ve
  - premium device_codes schema: jeffreysprompts_premium-h8r
  - premium CLI device code tests: jeffreysprompts_premium-wdao
- jeffreysprompts.com-hiff (CLI sync)
  - premium CLI sync endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-67o
- jeffreysprompts.com-wlg3 (CLI collections)
  - premium CLI collections endpoints (via sync): jeffreysprompts_premium-67o
- jeffreysprompts.com-xzgq (CLI offline/cache)
  - premium CLI sync endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-67o
- jeffreysprompts.com-t524 (CLI API docs)
  - premium CLI endpoints to document: jeffreysprompts_premium-67o, jeffreysprompts_premium-0o1, jeffreysprompts_premium-0ve, jeffreysprompts_premium-qnl
- jeffreysprompts.com-rd99 (CLI integration docs)
  - premium CLI auth + sync flows: jeffreysprompts_premium-0o1, jeffreysprompts_premium-qdm, jeffreysprompts_premium-mn0, jeffreysprompts_premium-qnl, jeffreysprompts_premium-0ve, jeffreysprompts_premium-67o

- jeffreysprompts_premium-6x3 (skills CRUD + install/export endpoints)
  - public CLI skills commands: jeffreysprompts.com-bojj
  - public CLI API docs: jeffreysprompts.com-t524
  - public CLI integration docs: jeffreysprompts.com-rd99


## Update Rules
- When adding a new CLI command, add the premium dependency here.
- When adding a new premium CLI endpoint, add the public CLI consumer here.

## Success Criteria
- Matrix added and cross-references are accurate.
- Future CLI changes use this matrix during planning.


## Cross-Repo Mirror
- Premium repo: jeffreysprompts_premium-zqsv

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-aod6","title":"Build public pricing page","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-onp.

## Task
Build public pricing page with plan comparison.

## Page: /pricing

### Header
- "Simple, Transparent Pricing"
- Subheadline about value

### Plan Cards
Two columns: Free (if applicable) vs Premium

**Free Tier** (if we have accounts without payment):
- Browse official prompts
- Basic search
- CLI access (read-only)
- "Current Plan" or "Get Started"

**Premium - $10/month**:
- Everything in Free
- Create unlimited prompts
- Prompt Packs & Skills
- Swap Meet access
- Collections
- Advanced search & filters
- Analytics dashboard
- Priority support
- "Subscribe Now" CTA

### Feature Comparison Table
Detailed grid showing all features by plan.

### FAQ Section
- "Can I cancel anytime?"
- "What payment methods?"
- "Is there a free trial?"
- "Team plans?"

### Trust Signals
- Secure payment via Stripe
- Cancel anytime
- 30-day money-back guarantee (if offered)

## Implementation
- Static page (can be MDX)
- Subscribe button → Stripe Checkout
- If logged in + subscribed, show "Manage Subscription"

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-z8ts","title":"Implement health check endpoints","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-kgv0. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Create health check endpoints for monitoring and load balancers.

## Why This Matters
- Load balancer health checks
- Kubernetes liveness/readiness
- Uptime monitoring integration
- Quick service status

## Endpoints

### 1. Basic Health (/health)
- Returns 200 if server running
- No external dependencies
- Used by load balancers
- Ultra-fast response

### 2. Ready Check (/health/ready)
- Checks database connection
- Checks Redis (if used)
- Checks critical services
- Returns 503 if not ready

### 3. Detailed Status (/health/status)
- Full system status
- Individual service status
- Version information
- Protected (admin only)

## Implementation

### Basic Health
```typescript
// src/app/api/health/route.ts
export async function GET() {
  return Response.json({ status: 'ok', timestamp: Date.now() });
}
```

### Ready Check
```typescript
// src/app/api/health/ready/route.ts
export async function GET() {
  const checks = await Promise.allSettled([
    checkDatabase(),
    checkSupabase(),
    checkStripe(),
  ]);
  
  const results = {
    database: checks[0].status === 'fulfilled',
    supabase: checks[1].status === 'fulfilled',
    stripe: checks[2].status === 'fulfilled',
  };
  
  const allHealthy = Object.values(results).every(Boolean);
  
  return Response.json(
    { status: allHealthy ? 'ready' : 'degraded', checks: results },
    { status: allHealthy ? 200 : 503 }
  );
}

async function checkDatabase() {
  const result = await db.execute(sql`SELECT 1`);
  if (!result) throw new Error('Database unreachable');
}
```

### Detailed Status (Admin)
```typescript
// src/app/api/health/status/route.ts
export async function GET(request: Request) {
  // Require admin auth
  const user = await authenticateRequest(request);
  if (!user?.isAdmin) return Response.json({ error: 'Forbidden' }, { status: 403 });
  
  return Response.json({
    version: process.env.APP_VERSION,
    environment: process.env.NODE_ENV,
    uptime: process.uptime(),
    memory: process.memoryUsage(),
    services: {
      database: await getDatabaseStatus(),
      supabase: await getSupabaseStatus(),
      stripe: await getStripeStatus(),
    },
    features: getFeatureFlags(),
  });
}
```

## Monitoring Integration
- Configure Vercel health checks
- Set up Better Uptime/Checkly
- PagerDuty alerting

## Files to Create
- src/app/api/health/route.ts
- src/app/api/health/ready/route.ts
- src/app/api/health/status/route.ts
- src/lib/health/checks.ts

## Success Criteria
- /health returns 200 quickly
- /health/ready checks dependencies
- Load balancer compatible
- Monitoring integrated

## Hardening Additions
- /health is dependency-free and cache-disabled.
- /health/ready should avoid leaking internals; return booleans only.
- /health/status remains admin-only; strip secrets.
- Add lightweight integration tests for status codes and JSON shape.

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-zosz","title":"Implement referral program for user growth","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-x85c.

## Task
Implement a referral program to encourage users to invite others.

## Why This Matters
- Viral growth loop (users bring users)
- Lower customer acquisition cost
- Higher quality leads (trusted referral)
- Rewards loyal users

## Program Structure

### Referral Rewards
**Referrer (existing user):**
- 1 month free Premium for each successful referral
- OR credit toward subscription
- Cap at 12 months free per year

**Referee (new user):**
- Extended trial (30 days instead of 14)
- OR 20% off first month

### Referral Flow
1. User gets unique referral link/code
2. Shares with friend
3. Friend signs up using link
4. Friend converts to paid
5. Both get rewards

### Referral Link
- Format: jeffreysprompts.com/r/[CODE]
- Unique per user
- Trackable

### Success Criteria for Referral
- Referee must convert to paid subscription
- Minimum 1 month paid to count
- No self-referral

## Database Schema
```typescript
export const referralCodes = pgTable("referral_codes", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  userId: uuid("user_id").references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  code: text("code").notNull().unique(),
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(),
});

export const referrals = pgTable("referrals", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  referrerId: uuid("referrer_id").references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  refereeId: uuid("referee_id").references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  codeUsed: text("code_used").notNull(),
  status: text("status").default("pending"), // pending, converted, rewarded
  referrerReward: text("referrer_reward"),
  refereeReward: text("referee_reward"),
  convertedAt: timestamp("converted_at"),
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(),
});
```

## API Endpoints
- GET /api/referral/code - Get user's referral code
- POST /api/referral/apply - Apply referral code on signup
- GET /api/referral/stats - Get referral statistics

## UI Components
- ReferralCard - Shows code and share buttons
- ReferralStats - Shows successful referrals
- ShareModal - Easy sharing to social/email

## Files to Create
- src/lib/db/schema/referrals.ts
- src/app/api/referral/route.ts
- src/app/(app)/referrals/page.tsx
- src/components/referral/referral-card.tsx
- src/components/referral/share-modal.tsx

## Success Criteria
- Referral tracking works
- Rewards applied correctly
- Easy to share
- Fraud prevention
